rulings.Case Title: JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL VS. SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Memorandum Of Costs Against Vikramsinh Dolatsinh Devdhara And Jaydipsinh Devdhara As A Result Of Dismissal Of Action Without Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENT SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Memorandum Of Costs Against Vikramsinh Dolatsinh Devdhara And Jaydipsinh Devdhara As A Result Of Dismissal Of Action Without Prejudice is continued to July 2, 2021 to be heard with the Motion to Tax Costs. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DOMINADOR G ESTEBAN VS. YEVA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT YEVA, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Hearing Required to address Notice of Stay of Proceedings re: Plaintiff's Bankruptcy. rulings.Case Title: NAIM JAMALI VS. JORGE ISABEL VILLATA ORDONEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTS KATHERINE ORDONEZ REYES, JORGE ORDONEZ VILLATA, JOSE GUZMAN SINTA and ALBA REYES SANDOVAL'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to: (1) either allege the correct number of pages attached to the complaint or attach all pages claimed to be attached to the complaint; (2) to reconcile allegations as to the parties to the lease with the parties to the lease indicated in the document attached. To the extent Defendant is demurring to a cause of action based on non-payment of rent, no such cause of action is alleged. Defendants should have filed a motion to strike irrelevant allegations, which appear to be have not been removed from the prior version of the complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
